subject: Mathematics
title: The Hotel with Infinite Rooms
description: A 60-second lesson on The Hotel with Infinite Rooms.

hook.text: Imagine a hotel with infinitely many rooms, all full. A new guest arrives. Can you fit them in?

buildup.text: Move every guest up one room: room 1 to room 2, room 2 to room 3, and so on forever.

discovery.text: Room 1 is now empty. The new guest checks in. An infinite hotel can always make room for one more.

twist.text: This only works because infinity plus one equals infinity. Finite hotels can't pull this trick.

climax.text: Hilbert's Hotel reveals that infinity doesn't obey the rules of everyday counting.

punchline.text: A full hotel with room for more—that's infinity.

quiz.question: How does Hilbert's Hotel accommodate a new guest when full?
options: Move every guest up one room
